data.body:
ROUND 6 — PRESSURE-TEST + parallel prior art + grounding (wf_7cfddf5f-265; 106 agents, 20 confirmed/5 REFUTED).
VERDICT: evidence SUPPORTS the converged stack, with ONE sharp caveat (agent-runtime structured-output path).

ENGINE (build thin interpreter) — VALIDATED + Joe's skepticism resolved with evidence. The "durable
execution as a library" prior art validates the in-process SHAPE but UNIFORMLY refutes adopting any of them:
DBOS owns its store (Postgres/SQLite IS the source of truth; MIT, ~1.4k*, v2.24 2026-06); Restate is
actually a SERVER (Rust binary + its own Bifrost log + RocksDB; the Python SDK is a thin client needing
`docker run restate`). NONE can defer persistence to an external git-backed event log. So building a thin
interpreter over rebar's own store is architecturally correct — the off-the-shelf wheels don't fit the
event-sourced AXLE, and the gap is structural not a landmine. DEEPEST UNVALIDATED RISK (open Q): resume/
replay correctness over an external event log (DBOS/Restate get correctness from OWNED transactional
stores; no prior art shows replay determinism over an external event log). => #1 engine de-risk POC.
[Refuted my provisional "DBOS does not own Postgres" — it DOES own it.]

DECLARATIVE IR + AGENT-STEP ENCODING — VALIDATED by Kestra: an entire agent invocation (provider-agnostic,
Claude first-class, tools + MCP over 4 transports) reduces to ONE compact declarative YAML node
(io.kestra.plugin.ai.agent.AIAgent; only prompt+provider required; Apache-2.0, v1.12 2026-06). JVM/server-
locked => LEARN the IR encoding from it, don't adopt. Concrete template for how rebar encodes agent steps.

VISUAL (ephemeral projection) — VALIDATED by cautionary tales: n8n's git is a LOSSY projection over its DB
(pulling causes brief service interruption/downtime); Windmill keeps TWO representations reconciled by CI/CD
("git wins" only by explicit config). The DB-vs-file dual-store reconciliation pain is exactly what rebar
sidesteps by making its store authoritative + the visual editor a NEVER-persisted projection. OPEN: bpmn-js/
draw.io embeddability + IR<->standard round-trip fidelity NOT verified => round-trip POC needed.

AGENT RUNTIME (Pydantic AI behind the Runner seam) — VALIDATED as seam-friendly (Pydantic AI's own DBOSAgent
wrapper proves the pluggable-runner-seam pattern is first-class). STRONGEST COUNTER-EVIDENCE (the #1 thing
to de-risk): Claude extended THINKING + forced-tool STRUCTURED OUTPUT is incompatible at the ANTHROPIC API
level (400: "Thinking may not be enabled when tool_choice forces tool use"); Pydantic AI's DEFAULT
structured mode (ToolOutput) forces tool_choice='any' -> hits it; AND anthropic-sdk-python #1204 (OPEN
Feb-Jun 2026, claude-sonnet-4-5/4-6): thinking+tools+json_schema structured output "broken or highly
unstable". CRUCIAL: this is API/SDK-level, NOT framework-specific — it ALSO affects rebar's CURRENT
LangGraph ToolStrategy path (codebase already documents that incompatibility). MITIGATION: use PromptedOutput
(not forced-tool ToolOutput), or Anthropic's GA grammar-constrained output_format (provider-native, no forced
tool_choice) — which STRENGTHENS the raw-anthropic-SDK+output_format option. SECONDARY: Pydantic AI churn
(~8-9 breaking 0.x releases Apr-Sep 2025; now 1.x stable BUT a 2.0 beta June 2026 = another wave) + a Jan-2026
maintainer-bandwidth crisis => ENFORCE the runner seam strictly so the dep stays swappable.
GOOD NEWS (refuted bad framings): "Langfuse-via-OTel from Pydantic AI is unsupported" REFUTED 0-3 (the
load-bearing premise for dropping LangChain survives); "Pydantic AI has no 2.0" refuted; "maintainer says AI
made them slower" refuted. OPEN: positively verify Pydantic-AI->Langfuse OTel TRACE FIDELITY to a non-Logfire
backend (load-bearing) => de-risk POC.

GROUNDING METHODOLOGY: promptfoo (exit-100 CI gating) complements rebar's existing Inspect AI evals; tau2-bench
as a tool-use benchmark reference. The 3 de-risk experiments (one per decision): (1) RUNTIME — golden-set eval
exercising Claude + structured output + extended thinking, asserting structured-output validity + zero-400
rate (+ verify Langfuse OTel traces land); (2) ENGINE — strangler-fig parallel-run-and-diff of the interpreter
vs recorded runs + a kill-mid-run/replay-from-events test proving no double-effects (resume/replay correctness);
(3) VISUAL — round-trip property test IR->editor->IR asserting semantic equality + agent-step metadata + ID
stability (bpmn-js vs draw.io), visual never persisted.

NET: keep the converged stack; (a) make the Runner seam non-negotiable (contains Pydantic AI churn), (b)
structured output via PromptedOutput / Anthropic output_format NOT forced-tool-choice (the #1 de-risk, and it
re-opens raw-SDK+output_format as a serious lighter alternative to Pydantic AI), (c) prove interpreter
replay-over-event-log empirically. Brainstorm core architecture is now grounded + pressure-tested.
